response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. This means she uses 2 ounces per week. In a leap year, there are 52 weeks and 2 days, so she uses 2 * 52 = 104 ounces of shampoo. She buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles, so she needs to buy [number_of_bottles] bottles to cover her needs.  

[address] is where she can purchase the shampoo. She needs to ensure that the total amount of shampoo she buys is at least 104 ounces. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so she needs to calculate how many bottles are required to meet her needs. The number of bottles needed is [number_of_bottles], which is calculated by dividing the total ounces needed by the ounces per bottle.
